mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is low and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, markets infer continued policy accommodation, reducing discount-rate pressure and supporting equity risk-taking over the next month. That mechanism predicts the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as term premia stay compressed.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward yield changes are positive in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below its 12-month average and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is flat or negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger household spending and nominal demand, which lifts growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises on a 3-month annualized basis above 5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by negative 20-day Treasury yield changes more often than positive ones, or if the average 20-day yield change is below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slower PAYEMS growth signals weakening labor demand and higher recession probability, which lowers expected real rates and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not beat either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone in out-of-sample directional accuracy, or if 20-day yield changes are not negative in at least 55% of instances with rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while HOUST remains weak, investors read the mix as tight capacity and inflation pressure rather than pure real growth, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month change turns positive and HOUST stays below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the signal is not positive in at least 55% of triggered cases or if yield changes are indistinguishable from zero despite the specified INDPRO-HOUST pattern.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp CPIAUCSL acceleration above policy rates raises expected inflation and term premia, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 5 trading days as bond investors reprice the path of real returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon yield changes are not positive after inflation exceeds policy rates, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is above FEDFUNDS but still decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 alongside softer RSAFS confirms broad final-demand weakness, which lowers growth expectations and drives the 10-year Treasury yield down over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth is negative and RSAFS is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDP-and-sales episodes do not produce lower 60-day yields on average, or if the hit rate for downward yield moves is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: If UNRATE is already elevated and PAYEMS continues to decelerate, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that should dominate near-term inflation concerns and pull the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is above 4.5% and PAYEMS 3-month average growth is below zero
failure_condition: Reject if high-unemployment and declining-payroll episodes fail to generate negative 20-day yield changes, or if yield declines are not larger than in periods with only one labor signal active.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO together with firmer HOUST signals stronger supply-side activity and better medium-run growth capacity, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days as investors mark up the neutral rate.
failure_condition: Reject if paired improvements in industrial production and housing starts do not precede positive 60-day yield changes, or if the conditional mean yield change is not above zero over multiple recovery episodes.
